title: pandanus butyrophorus
intro: Pandanus butyrophorus, also known as the Butter Tree or Malabar Padauk, is a species of plant in the family Pandanaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and bark of the Pandanus butyrophorus plant possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat various health issues.
